# services.livekit

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.livekit`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.livekit.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the livekit server. |
| `services.livekit.ingress.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the livekit ingress service. |
| `services.livekit.ingress.environmentFile` | `null or absolute path` | Environment file as defined in {manpage}`systemd.exec(5)` passed to the service. Use this to specify `LIVEKIT_API_KEY` and `LIVEKIT_API_SECRET`. |
| `services.livekit.ingress.openFirewall.rtc` | `boolean` | Open WebRTC ports in the firewall. |
| `services.livekit.ingress.openFirewall.rtmp` | `boolean` | Open RTMP port in the firewall. |
| `services.livekit.ingress.openFirewall.whip` | `boolean` | Open WHIP port in the firewall. |
| `services.livekit.ingress.package` | `package` | The livekit-ingress package to use. |
| `services.livekit.ingress.settings` | `open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)` | LiveKit Ingress configuration. See <https://github.com/livekit/ingress?tab=readme-ov-file#config> for possible options. |
| `services.livekit.ingress.settings.redis.address` | `null or string` | Address or hostname and port for redis connection |
| `services.livekit.ingress.settings.rtc_config.port_range_end` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | End of UDP port range for WebRTC |
| `services.livekit.ingress.settings.rtc_config.port_range_start` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Start of UDP port range for WebRTC |
| `services.livekit.ingress.settings.rtc_config.use_external_ip` | `boolean` | When set to true, attempts to discover the host's public IP via STUN. This is useful for cloud environments such as AWS & Google where hosts have an internal IP that maps to an external one. |
| `services.livekit.ingress.settings.rtmp_port` | `integer between -1 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | TCP port for RTMP connections. -1 to disable |
| `services.livekit.ingress.settings.whip_port` | `integer between -1 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | TCP port for WHIP connections. -1 to disable |
| `services.livekit.keyFile` | `absolute path` | LiveKit key file holding one or multiple application secrets. Use `livekit-server generate-keys` to generate a random key name and secret. The file should have the format `<keyname>: <secret>`. Example: `lk-jwt-service: f6lQGaHtM5HfgZjIcec3cOCRfiDqIine4CpZZnqdT5cE` Individual key/secret pairs need to be passed to clients to connect to this instance. |
| `services.livekit.openFirewall` | `boolean` | Opens port range for LiveKit on the firewall. |
| `services.livekit.package` | `package` | The livekit package to use. |
| `services.livekit.redis.createLocally` | `boolean` | Whether to set up a local redis instance. |
| `services.livekit.redis.host` | `null or string` | Address to bind local redis instance to. |
| `services.livekit.redis.port` | `null or 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port to bind local redis instance to. |
| `services.livekit.settings` | `open submodule of (JSON value)` | LiveKit configuration file expressed in nix. For an example configuration, see <https://docs.livekit.io/home/self-hosting/deployment/#configuration>. For all possible values, see <https://github.com/livekit/livekit/blob/master/config-sample.yaml>. |
| `services.livekit.settings.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Main TCP port for RoomService and RTC endpoint. |
| `services.livekit.settings.redis.address` | `null or string` | Host and port used to connect to a redis instance. |
| `services.livekit.settings.rtc.port_range_end` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | End of UDP port range for WebRTC |
| `services.livekit.settings.rtc.port_range_start` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Start of UDP port range for WebRTC |
| `services.livekit.settings.rtc.use_external_ip` | `boolean` | When set to true, attempts to discover the host's public IP via STUN. This is useful for cloud environments such as AWS & Google where hosts have an internal IP that maps to an external one. |
